problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement Rerank in the Novita Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. The Novita Go driver in internal/entity/models/novita.go has a stub Rerank method that always returns
"novita, no such method", even though Novita publicly documents a Cohere-compatible rerank endpoint at:

  POST https://api.novita.ai/openai/v1/rerank

(see https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-rerank)

The endpoint accepts the same Bearer token the chat and embeddings paths already use, takes the standard
{model, query, documents, top_n} request body, and returns a results array with index and relevance_score
fields the cluster's RerankResponse type already expects.

After #14850 (Go: implement provider: Novita.ai) and #14895 (Go: implement Embed in Novita driver) merged,
Novita supports chat and embeddings end to end in the Go layer. Rerank is the natural next gap: a tenant
running a Novita-only RAG stack still has to switch providers for the rerank step, even though their
Novita key already covers it.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Implement `NovitaModel.Rerank` in `internal/entity/models/novita.go` so Novita rerank models work end to end, and add the rerank URL suffix and rerank model entries to `conf/models/novita.json`.

The driver should:

- POST to `${BaseURL}/${URLSuffix.Rerank}` (resolves to `https://api.novita.ai/openai/v1/rerank` with default config — see https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-rerank).
- Send `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`, the same header the chat and embeddings paths already use.
- Build the Cohere-compatible request body:

```json
{
  "model": "<model_name>",
  "query": "<query_text>",
  "documents": ["<passage_0>", "<passage_1>"],
  "top_n": <len(documents)>
}
```

- Parse the documented response shape. Each item in `results` is wrapped with a `document` object — the driver only needs `index` and `relevance_score`:

```json
{
  "results": [
    {"document": {"text": "<passage_0>"}, "index": 0, "relevance_score": 0.97},
    {"document": {"text": "<passage_1>"}, "index": 1, "relevance_score": 0.14}
  ]
}
```

  Map each item to `RerankResult{Index: index, RelevanceScore: relevance_score}` and return as `*RerankResponse`. The `document.text` wrapper can be ignored — `RerankResponse` doesn't surface the source text back to the caller.

- Default `top_n` to `len(documents)` so every input gets a score returned; honor `RerankConfig.TopN` when the caller passes a smaller value.
- Use …

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement provider: GPUStack in Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. GPUStack is listed as unchecked in the Go-rewrite tracker #14736, and `internal/service/llm.go:171` already classifies it as a self-deployed provider alongside Ollama, Xinference, LocalAI, and LM Studio — but `internal/entity/models/` has no `gpustack.go` driver, so the new Go API server cannot route GPUStack instances. The Python side already supports GPUStack chat through the LiteLLM `openai/` route (`rag/llm/chat_model.py:1872`, `<base>/v1/chat/completions`).
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Add chat support for GPUStack as a new `GPUStackModel` in `internal/entity/models/gpustack.go`, plus  conf/models/gpustack.json`, `gpustack_test.go`, and a factory registration. GPUStack is self-hosted and OpenAI-compatible (https://docs.gpustack.ai/):

- chat: `${base}/v1/chat/completions` with `{model, messages, stream}` and   `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. SSE stream parsing.
- list models: `${base}/v1/models`.
- check connection: delegate to ListModels.

Base URL is user-supplied at instance creation time (no default), matching the existing self-hosted Go drivers: LM Studio (#14586), vLLM (#14532), Ollama (#14580), LocalAI (#14813), Xinference (#14938). 
Follow-on issues will add Embed and Rerank, in line with how Novita, NVIDIA, TogetherAI, and other providers landed method-by-method.

### Describe implementation you've considered

_No response_

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
No new docs needed. Makes Add-Provider work for GPUStack through the Go API
server, the same way it already works for LM Studio, vLLM, Ollama, LocalAI, and
Xinference.
```

### Additional information

Tracked under #14736. Self-hosted-provider cluster: LM Studio #14586, vLLM #14532, Ollama #14580, LocalAI #14813,  inference #14938. `selfDeployed` map at `internal/service/llm.go:165-172` already includes `"GPUStack": true`, expecting a driver to exist.

problems.title: [Bug]: IDOR — cross-tenant KB exposure via POST /api/v1/dify/retrieval
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

eacec8650

### RAGFlow image version

v0.25.4

### Other environment information

```Markdown

```

### Actual behavior

`HTTP 200` with the contents of A's private chunks:

```json
{
  "records": [
    {
      "content": "... VICTIM_SECRET_MARKER_42 ...",
      "score": 0.83,
      "title": "victim_doc.txt",
      "metadata": {
        "doc_id": "0e1a...",
        "document_id": "0e1a...",
        "author": "alice@victim.example",
        "internal_id": "ACME-2026-Q2-PRIVATE"
      }
    },
    {
      "content": "... second chunk from A's document ...",
      "score": 0.71,
      "title": "victim_doc.txt",
      "metadata": { "doc_id": "0e1a...", "document_id": "0e1a..." }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Expected behavior

The endpoint must return 403 RetCode.AUTHENTICATION_ERROR ("No authorization." or equivalent) when the caller's API-key tenant is not allowed to access the requested knowledge base — matching the check used by every other tenant-aware route in api/db/services/knowledgebase_service.py:485.

### Steps to reproduce

```Markdown
### Steps to reproduce

Reproduces against any deployment exposing `/api/v1/dify/retrieval` (Dify
External Knowledge integration enabled) with at least two tenants.

1. **Set up tenant A (victim).**
   - Sign in as user A; create a knowledge base, e.g. `victim_kb`.
   - Upload a document with identifiable content (e.g. a `.txt` containing the
     string `"VICTIM_SECRET_MARKER_42"`) and let it finish parsing.
   - Copy the KB id from the URL (`/dataset/<kb_id>/...`) or from a network
     trace. Call it `<KB_A_ID>`.

2. **Set up tenant B (attacker, separate account).**
   - Sign in as user B (any RAGFlow account *other than* A; can be a free
     self-registered account on the same deployment).
   - Navigate to **Profile → API Keys** and click **Create new key**. Copy
     the token. Call it `<B_API_KEY>`.

3. **Confirm B cannot reach A's KB through the normal UI.**
   - Verify that the `victim_kb` is not listed under B's datasets — confirming
     the standard authorization boundary holds for the UI.

4. **Exploit via the unauthenticated-for-tenant Dify retrieval endpoint.**
   - With B's API key, call A's KB by id:

     
     curl -sS -X POST "https://<ragflow-host>/api/v1/dify/retrieval" \
       -H "Authorization: Bearer <B_API_KEY>" \
       -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
       -d '{
         "knowledge_id": "<KB_A_ID>",
         "query": "VICTIM_SEC…

problems.title: [Bug]: Custom base URL model requests can panic when Go drivers return nil from NewInstance
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

`0a0bae2e288aa26b2978a31924cba0ea74dfc5c3`

### RAGFlow image version

`main` branch at commit `0a0bae2e288aa26b2978a31924cba0ea74dfc5c3`; not image-version specific.

### Other environment information

- Hardware parameters: N/A
- OS type: N/A
- Others: Reproducible from the Go source code in `internal/entity/models/*.go` and `internal/service/model_service.go`.

### Actual behavior

Several `ModelProviderService` paths create request-local drivers for custom `base_url` model instances by calling `NewInstance(newURL)` and then immediately invoking a driver method on the returned value.

Examples on current `main`:

```go
driver = driver.NewInstance(newURL)
return driver.ListModels(apiConfig)
```

and in active/local model paths:

```go
newProviderInfo := providerInfo.ModelDriver.NewInstance(newURL)
response, err = newProviderInfo.ChatWithMessages(modelName, messages, apiConfig, modelConfig)
```

However, several real Go model drivers still return `nil` from `NewInstance`, including:

- `AliyunModel`
- `DeepSeekModel`
- `GiteeModel`
- `GoogleModel`
- `MinimaxModel`
- `MoonshotModel`
- `SiliconflowModel`
- `VolcEngine`
- `ZhipuAIModel`

When one of these providers is used with a custom `base_url`, the service can call a method on a nil `ModelDriver` and panic instead of returning a normal error.

### Expected behavior

Custom base URL handling should not be able to panic.

Either each real provider that supports custom `base_url` should return a valid configured driver from `NewInstance`, or the service should detect a nil/unsupported `NewInstance` result and return a clear validation error.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use `main` at commit `0a0bae2e288aa26b2978a31924cba0ea74dfc5c3`.
2. Configure one affected provider, for example DeepSeek, Google, Aliyun, or Gitee, with an instance `extra.base_url`.
3. Trigger a path that uses the custom base URL, such as listing supported models, checking provider connection, listing/showing tasks, or calling an active/local chat/embedding/rerank path.
4. Observe that `internal/service/model_service.go` calls `NewInstance(newURL)`.
5. Observe that the affected provider's `NewInstance` returns `nil`, after which the service invokes a method on the nil driver.

### Additional information

I searched open and closed issues/PRs for related terms including `NewInstance`, `NewInstance nil`, `NewInstance base_url ModelDriver`, and `custom base URL`.

The closest relat…

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement rerank in the Xinference Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. The Xinference Go chat provider is tracked in #14808, but the initial chat-provider scope leaves Rerank as a stub that returns "no such method".

Xinference supports rerank models through a dedicated `/v1/rerank` endpoint. The official docs describe a request shape with `model`, `query`, and `documents`, and the built-in model docs show rerank models are launched separately with `--model-type rerank`. That means RAGFlow should implement rerank only for Xinference rerank-capable models, without treating chat LLMs as rerankers.

Without this follow-up, a tenant can configure Xinference for chat in the Go layer but cannot use a Xinference-hosted rerank model to improve retrieval ranking.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Implement `XinferenceModel.Rerank` in `internal/entity/models/xinference.go` so rerank models served by a tenant's Xinference instance work end to end.

The driver should:

- POST to `/v1/rerank` on the normalized tenant-supplied Xinference base URL.
- Accept a model name / model UID, query string, and list of documents.
- Send the documented request shape: `model`, `query`, `documents`, and `top_n` when configured.
- Return a `RerankResponse` with each result's document index and relevance score.
- Validate response indices so duplicate or out-of-range indices fail clearly.
- Use the same optional-auth behavior as the chat provider: only set `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` when a non-empty key is configured.
- Use a bounded non-streaming request timeout, matching the existing provider patterns.

The provider config should include the rerank suffix:

```json
"url_suffix": {
  "chat": "chat/completions",
  "models": "models",
  "embedding": "embeddings",
  "rerank": "rerank"
}
```

No new public API. No ModelDriver interface change. This is a follow-up implementation behind the existing `Rerank` method.

### Describe implementation you've considered

Follow the existing Go rerank implementations where possible, while preserving Xinference-specific behavior:

- normalize `http://host:9997` and `http://host:9997/v1` to the same `/v1` base before appending `rerank`;
- keep API key optional because default Xinference deployments run without auth;
- support the documented Xinference rerank request fields only;
- do not treat chat LLMs as rerank models, because Xinference launches rerank models separately with `--model-type rerank`.

### Documentat…

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement provider: ModelScope in Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).

- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. ModelScope is listed as unchecked in the Go-rewrite tracker #14736, and `conf/llm_factories.json:992` already registers a `ModelScope` factory (`tags: "LLM"`, `status: "1"`) — but `internal/entity/models/` has no `modelscope.go` driver and `conf/models/` has no `modelscope.json`, so the new Go API server cannot route ModelScope instances. The Python side already supports ModelScope through the OpenAI-compatible base at `rag/llm/chat_model.py:618` (`ModelScopeChat`), which requires a user-supplied `base_url` and appends `/v1`.

Additionally, ModelScope is missing from the `selfDeployed` map at `internal/service/llm.go:165-172` (currently lists FastEmbed, Ollama, Xinference, LocalAI, LM-Studio, GPUStack). The Python implementation requires a user-supplied URL with no default, matching the self-hosted pattern, so the Go side should classify it the same way.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Add chat support for ModelScope as a new `ModelScopeModel` in `internal/entity/models/modelscope.go`, plus `conf/models/modelscope.json`, `modelscope_test.go`, a factory registration in `internal/entity/models/factory.go`, and an entry in the `selfDeployed` map at `internal/service/llm.go:171`. ModelScope is self-hosted / OpenAI-API-compatible (https://modelscope.ai/docs/openapi):

- chat: `${base}/v1/chat/completions` with `{model, messages, stream}` and `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. SSE stream parsing.
- list models: `${base}/v1/models`.
- check connection: delegate to ListModels.

Base URL is user-supplied at instance creation time (no default), matching the existing self-hosted Go drivers: LM Studio (#14586), vLLM (#14532), Ollama (#14580), LocalAI (#14813), Xinference (#14938), GPUStack (#15023). The Python class enforces this with `if not base_url: raise ValueError("Local llm url cannot be None")` at `chat_model.py:621`.
Follow-on issues will add Embed and Rerank, in line with how Novita, NVIDIA, TogetherAI, and other providers landed method-by-method.

### Describe implementation you've considered

_No response_

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
No new docs needed. Makes Add-Provider work for ModelScope through the Go API
server, the same way it already works for LM Studio, vLLM, Ollama, LocalAI,
Xinference, and (pending #15023) GPUStack.
```

### Additional information

problems.title: [Bug]: Custom base_url is inconsistently resolved when model instance region is empty
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

`6499bce2a67c55191efb7dc4cfe3ff359b64c5ef`

### RAGFlow image version

`main` branch at commit `6499bce2a67c55191efb7dc4cfe3ff359b64c5ef`; not image-version specific.

### Other environment information

- Hardware parameters: N/A
- OS type: N/A
- Others: Reproducible from the Go source code in `internal/service/model_service.go` and `internal/entity/models/*.go`.

### Actual behavior

Custom `base_url` handling for Go model drivers is inconsistent when a model instance has no configured region.

After #15030, `ModelProviderService` creates request-local drivers through `newModelDriverForBaseURL`. The helper preserves the configured region key:

```go
newDriver := driver.NewInstance(map[string]string{
    region: baseURL,
})
```

At the service call sites, `region` comes from `extra["region"]` and is also assigned to `apiConfig.Region`:

```go
region := extra["region"]
apiConfig.Region = &region
```

When `extra["region"]` is missing or empty, the request-local driver's base URL map is keyed by the empty string:

```go
BaseURL[""] = customBaseURL
```

Some driver methods then preserve the empty region and read `BaseURL[""]`, so the custom URL works. Examples:

- `internal/entity/models/openai.go`: `ChatWithMessages` and `ListModels`
- `internal/entity/models/ollama.go`: synchronous `ChatWithMessages`
- `internal/entity/models/lmstudio.go`: synchronous `ChatWithMessages`

Other driver methods normalize an empty region to `"default"` before looking up the base URL, so they read `BaseURL["default"]` instead. Examples:

- `internal/entity/models/openai.go`: streaming chat and embedding paths
- `internal/entity/models/vllm.go`: chat, streaming chat, embedding, list models, and check connection paths
- `internal/entity/models/localai.go`: paths using `resolveBaseURL`
- `internal/entity/models/xinference.go`: paths using `xinferenceRegion` / `baseURLForRegion`
- `internal/entity/models/ollama.go` and `internal/entity/models/lmstudio.go`: streaming, embedding, and list models paths

For those `"default"` lookup paths, a custom `base_url` configured without a region can be ignored or reported as missing even though the service already supplied it under `BaseURL[""]`.

### Expected behavior

A custom `base_url` with no explicit region should resolve consistently across all methods of a provider.

Either the service should provide a default-region fallback for empty-region custom URLs, or all drivers shoul…

Avian is listed as unchecked in the Go-rewrite tracker #14736, and `conf/llm_factories.json:6733` already registers an `Avian` factory with 4 preconfigured chat models (deepseek-v3.2, kimi-k2.5, glm-5, minimax-m2.5) — but `internal/entity/models/` has no `avian.go` driver and `conf/models/` has no `avian.json`, so the new Go API server cannot route Avian instances. The Python side already supports Avian chat at `rag/llm/chat_model.py:1220` (`AvianChat`), routed through the LiteLLM `openai/` provider per `rag/llm/__init__.py:61, 92, 135`, with default base URL `https://api.avian.io/v1`.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Add chat support for Avian as a new `AvianModel` in `internal/entity/models/avian.go`, plus `conf/models/avian.json`, `avian_test.go`, and a factory registration in `internal/entity/models/factory.go`. Avian is a SaaS, OpenAI-API-compatible inference platform (https://api.avian.io/docs/):

- chat: `${base}/v1/chat/completions` with `{model, messages, stream}` and `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. SSE stream parsing.
- list models: `${base}/v1/models`.
- check connection: delegate to ListModels.

Base URL defaults to `https://api.avian.io/v1` with user-supplied override, matching the existing SaaS Go drivers: Novita (#14850), TogetherAI (#14957), Replicate (#14958), DeepInfra (#14978), Upstage (#14819), LongCat (#14809). The Python class enforces the default at `chat_model.py:1223`.
Follow-on issues will add Embed and Rerank, in line with how Novita, NVIDIA, TogetherAI, and other providers landed method-by-method.

### Describe implementation you've considered

_No response_

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
No new docs needed. Makes Add-Provider work for Avian through the Go API
server, the same way it already works for Novita, TogetherAI, Replicate,
DeepInfra, Upstage, and LongCat.
```

problems.title: [Bug]: SDK routes crash when Authorization header is missing
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

`6499bce2a67c55191efb7dc4cfe3ff359b64c5ef`

### RAGFlow image version

`main` branch at commit `6499bce2a67c55191efb7dc4cfe3ff359b64c5ef`; not image-version specific.

### Other environment information

- Hardware parameters: N/A
- OS type: N/A
- Others: Reproducible from the SDK API source code in `api/apps/sdk/session.py`.

### Actual behavior

Several SDK routes call `.split()` directly on the return value of `request.headers.get("Authorization")`:

```python
token = request.headers.get("Authorization").split()
```

If the `Authorization` header is missing, `request.headers.get("Authorization")` returns `None`, so these routes raise `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'split'` before they can return the intended `Authorization is not valid!` response.

Current affected routes in `api/apps/sdk/session.py` include:

- `POST /chatbots/<dialog_id>/completions`
- `GET /chatbots/<dialog_id>/info`
- `POST /agentbots/<agent_id>/completions`
- `GET /agentbots/<agent_id>/inputs`
- `POST /searchbots/ask`
- `POST /searchbots/retrieval_test`
- `POST /searchbots/related_questions`
- `GET /searchbots/detail`
- `POST /searchbots/mindmap`

### Expected behavior

SDK routes should handle missing, empty, or malformed `Authorization` headers without raising an exception. They should return the existing authentication error response consistently, for example `Authorization is not valid!`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use `main` at commit `6499bce2a67c55191efb7dc4cfe3ff359b64c5ef`.
2. Send a request to an SDK route without an `Authorization` header, for example:

```bash
curl -X GET "http://localhost:<port>/chatbots/<dialog_id>/info"
```

or:

```bash
curl -X POST "http://localhost:<port>/searchbots/related_questions" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"question":"hello"}'
```

3. Observe that the code calls `.split()` on `None`.
4. The route raises an internal error instead of returning the intended authentication error response.

### Additional information

I searched open and closed issues/PRs for related terms including `api/apps/sdk/session.py Authorization split`, `SDK Authorization split`, `missing Authorization SDK endpoint`, and `Authorization header session.py`.

The closest related PR I found is #14592, which fixed tenant-scoped authorization for chatbot SDK endpoints. That PR did not handle missing `Authorization` headers before `.split()`, and the raw `.split()` pattern r…

problems.title: [Bug]: /documents/{doc_id}/download sets Content-Type from default ext=markdown when ext is omitted
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

`b28e134944bffbb0de16efac1a67d5de9a3ad7ab`

### RAGFlow image version

Observed from **source** at the commit above (not tied to a specific Docker tag).

### Other environment information

- Hardware parameters: N/A  
- OS type: N/A  
- Others: Issue is visible from static review of `api/apps/restful_apis/document_api.py` and is reproducible with any deployment when calling the REST API without the `ext` query parameter.

### Actual behavior

`GET /api/v1/documents/<doc_id>/download` uses a query parameter `ext` that defaults to `"markdown"` when omitted:

```python
ext = request.args.get("ext", "markdown")
content_type = CONTENT_TYPE_MAP.get(ext, f"application/{ext}")
```

So a client that downloads the raw bytes **without** passing `?ext=...` (e.g. `curl` or a generic HTTP client) receives `Content-Type: text/markdown` even when the underlying object is a PDF, Office document, image, etc.

The sibling **`GET /api/v1/documents/<doc_id>/preview`** endpoint instead resolves the extension from `doc.name` and sets `Content-Type` accordingly, so the two routes disagree for the same document when `ext` is not supplied on download.

### Expected behavior

When `ext` is not provided, the download response should use the same extension / MIME inference as preview (e.g. derive from the document’s stored filename or metadata), or the API should document `ext` as **required** and reject requests without it. Either way, the default should not silently label arbitrary binaries as `text/markdown`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use a document whose stored file is not markdown (e.g. `.pdf`), and note its `doc_id`.
2. Call:
   ```bash
   curl -sSI -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
     "http://<host>/api/v1/documents/<doc_id>/download"
   ```
   (omit the `ext` query parameter entirely.)
3. Observe `Content-Type` is `text/markdown; charset=utf-8` (or similar) from the default `ext=markdown` path.
4. Compare with:
   ```bash
   curl -sSI -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
     "http://<host>/api/v1/documents/<doc_id>/preview"
   ```
   which sets MIME type based on the document filename.

### Additional information

- `example/sdk` and internal test helpers often pass `ext` explicitly (e.g. `test_common.document_download(..., ext=...)` defaults to `"markdown"` in tests), which can hide this behavior for API users who do not send `ext`.
- Related UI path: `web/src/services/file-manager-service.ts` passes `para…

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement ASR in the OpenRouter Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. OpenRouter now exposes an official speech-to-text endpoint at:

  POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/audio/transcriptions

The existing Go driver already supports OpenRouter TTS through `AudioSpeech`,
but `OpenRouterModel.TranscribeAudio` in `internal/entity/models/openrouter.go`
still returns `openrouter, no such method`.

This leaves OpenRouter ASR models unavailable through the Go API server even
though the provider has an official transcription API and OpenRouter audio
support is already partially implemented.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Implement `OpenRouterModel.TranscribeAudio` so OpenRouter speech-to-text models can be used through the Go model driver.

- Add `"asr": "audio/transcriptions"` to `conf/models/openrouter.json`
- Add an OpenRouter transcription model entry, for example `openai/whisper-large-v3` with `model_types: ["asr"]`
- Read the local audio file and base64-encode it
- Send the documented JSON body with:
  - `model`
  - `input_audio.data`
  - `input_audio.format`
  - optional ASR params such as `language`, `temperature`, and `provider`
- Return the response `text` as `ASRResponse.Text`
- Surface non-200 responses with upstream status and body

No `ModelDriver` interface change is needed.

### Describe implementation you've considered

Follow the same Go model-provider capability pattern used by recent merged ASR/TTS PRs, including focused `httptest` coverage for request shape, input validation, response parsing, and upstream error surfacing.

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
No new public RAGFlow API is required. This enables the existing Go model
provider abstraction to call OpenRouter's official transcription endpoint.

Relevant OpenRouter docs:
- https://openrouter.ai/docs/api/api-reference/transcriptions/create-audio-transcriptions
- https://openrouter.ai/announcements/announcing-audio-apis
```

### Additional information

This follows the same recently merged provider-capability pattern as:

- #14926: Go: implement TTS for fishaudio, openrouter and asr for fishaudio
- #15096: Go: implement ASR and TTS for Xinference
- #15107: Go: implement rerank, asr, tts for TogetherAI

problems.title: [Bug]: ession User ID Spoofing via Attacker-Controlled `user_id` in POST /api/v1/chats/<chat_id>/sessions
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

`dc01e0e51` (current HEAD on `main` as of 2026-05-20)

### RAGFlow image version

Affects all versions containing `api/apps/restful_apis/chat_api.py` — confirm against your build (e.g., shown on the `System` page in the UI).

### Other environment information

```Markdown
- **Affected endpoint:** `POST /api/v1/chats/<chat_id>/sessions`
- **Affected file:** `api/apps/restful_apis/chat_api.py`, line 681
- **Auth required:** Yes — any authenticated user can trigger this
- **Severity:** Critical (OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control)

---
```

### Actual behavior

The `create_session` endpoint accepts an optional `user_id` field from the request body and writes it directly to the database without validating that it belongs to the authenticated user:

```python
# api/apps/restful_apis/chat_api.py:681
"user_id": req.get("user_id", current_user.id),  # ← attacker-controlled
```

An authenticated attacker who owns any `chat_id` can submit:

```http
POST /api/v1/chats/<attacker_chat_id>/sessions
Content-Type: application/json

{"user_id": "<victim_user_id>"}
```

The session is persisted to the database under the victim's `user_id`. This bypasses all identity checks — the only authorization performed is that `chat_id` belongs to the current user, not that `user_id` does.

**Exploit chain:**

1. Attacker authenticates and creates a chat → obtains `chat_id`.
2. POSTs `{"user_id": "<victim_id>"}` → session saved attributed to the victim.
3. `GET /api/v1/chats/<chat_id>/sessions?user_id=<victim_id>` returns those sessions, confirming successful attribution.

---

### Expected behavior

`user_id` in the created session must always be set to `current_user.id`, regardless of what the request body contains. An authenticated user must never be able to create records attributed to another identity.

---

### Steps to reproduce

```Markdown
1. Authenticate as User A and note your `user_id`.
2. Create a chat via `POST /api/v1/chats` — note the returned `chat_id`.
3. Obtain any valid `user_id` for User B (e.g., from a shared/public profile or API response).
4. Send:
   
   curl -X POST https://<host>/api/v1/chats/<chat_id>/sessions \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer <token_A>" \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"user_id": "<user_id_B>"}'
   
5. Confirm the session was created by querying:
   
   curl "https://<host>/api/v1/chats/<chat_id>/sessions?user_id=<user_id_B>" \
     -H "Authorization: Bear…

### Documentation, adoption, use case

No new docs needed. Makes Add-Provider work for Groq through the Go API server,
the same way it already works for Astraflow, Avian, Novita, TogetherAI,
Replicate, DeepInfra, Upstage, and LongCat.
```

### Additional information

Tracked under #14736. SaaS-provider cluster: Astraflow #15062, Avian #15044, Novita #14850, TogetherAI #14957, Replicate #14958, DeepInfra #14978, Upstage #14819, LongCat #14809. `conf/llm_factories.json:1631` already declares the `Groq` factory with 8 preconfigured chat models, expecting a driver to exist.

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement provider: PPIO in Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. PPIO is listed as unchecked in the Go-rewrite tracker #14736, and `conf/llm_factories.json:3698` already registers a `PPIO` factory (`tags: "LLM"`) with 19 preconfigured chat models — but `internal/entity/models/` has no `ppio.go` driver and `conf/models/` has no `ppio.json`, so the new Go API server cannot route PPIO instances. The Python side already supports PPIO through the unified `LiteLLMBase` dispatcher (`rag/llm/chat_model.py:1265`+, enum `PPIO` in `rag/llm/__init__.py:45`, default base `https://api.ppinfra.com/v3/openai`, LiteLLM prefix `openai/`).
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Add chat support for PPIO as a new `PPIOModel` in `internal/entity/models/ppio.go`, plus `conf/models/ppio.json`, `ppio_test.go`, and a factory registration in `internal/entity/models/factory.go`. PPIO exposes an OpenAI-API-compatible REST surface at `api.ppinfra.com/v3/openai` (https://ppio.com/docs/get-started/quickstart):

- chat: `${base}/chat/completions` with `{model, messages, stream}` and `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. SSE stream parsing.
- list models: `${base}/models`.
- check connection: delegate to ListModels.

Note that PPIO's OpenAI-compatible base path is `/v3/openai`, not the more common `/v1`, so the shipped `conf/models/ppio.json` should set `"url": "https://api.ppinfra.com/v3/openai"` and `"url_suffix": { "chat": "chat/completions", "models": "models" }`. Base URL is user-overridable, matching the existing SaaS Go drivers: Astraflow (#15062), Avian (#15044), Novita (#14850), TogetherAI (#14957), Replicate (#14958), DeepInfra (#14978), Upstage (#14819), LongCat (#14809).

### Describe implementation you've considered

_No response_

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
No new docs needed. Makes Add-Provider work for PPIO through the Go API server,
the same way it already works for Astraflow, Avian, Novita, TogetherAI,
Replicate, DeepInfra, Upstage, and LongCat.
```

### Additional information

Tracked under #14736. SaaS-provider cluster: Astraflow #15062, Avian #15044, Novita #14850, TogetherAI #14957, Replicate #14958, DeepInfra #14978, Upstage #14819, LongCat #14809. `conf/llm_factories.json:3698` already declares the `PPIO` factory with 19 preconfigured chat models, expecting a driver to exist.

problems.title: [Bug]: OpenAI Go driver advertises ASR/TTS models but returns no such method
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

`111cdc77b55c76d68352ed498594948dddb0e8d6`

### RAGFlow image version

`main` branch at commit `111cdc77b55c76d68352ed498594948dddb0e8d6`; not image-version specific.

### Other environment information

- Hardware parameters: N/A
- OS type: N/A
- Others: Reproducible from `conf/models/openai.json`, `internal/entity/models/openai.go`, and the generic Go model endpoints in `internal/handler/providers.go`.

### Actual behavior

`conf/models/openai.json` advertises OpenAI audio models:

- `whisper-1` with `model_types: ["asr"]`
- `tts-1` with `model_types: ["tts"]`

The Go provider API also exposes generic audio endpoints:

- `POST /api/v1/audio/transcriptions`
- `POST /api/v1/audio/speech`

However, the Go OpenAI driver returns `no such method` for both advertised capabilities:

```go
func (o *OpenAIModel) TranscribeAudio(...) (*ASRResponse, error) {
    return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s, no such method", o.Name())
}

func (o *OpenAIModel) AudioSpeech(...) (*TTSResponse, error) {
    return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s, no such method", o.Name())
}
```

The streaming variants also return `no such method`. In addition, `conf/models/openai.json` does not define `url_suffix.asr` or `url_suffix.tts` even though those model types are present.

As a result, users can select configured OpenAI ASR/TTS models, but the corresponding Go endpoints fail immediately with `openai, no such method`.

### Expected behavior

Advertised OpenAI ASR/TTS models should be implemented by the Go driver, or the unsupported models should not be advertised as usable Go provider capabilities.

A practical implementation would add the missing URL suffixes and driver methods for the documented OpenAI endpoints:

- ASR: `audio/transcriptions` for `whisper-1`
- TTS: `audio/speech` for `tts-1`

The generic Go audio endpoints should then call OpenAI successfully instead of returning `no such method`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run RAGFlow from `main` at commit `111cdc77b55c76d68352ed498594948dddb0e8d6`.
2. Configure an OpenAI provider instance.
3. Call `POST /api/v1/audio/transcriptions` with `provider_name: "OpenAI"` and `model_name: "whisper-1"`.
4. Observe that `whisper-1` is advertised as an ASR model in `conf/models/openai.json`.
5. Observe that `OpenAIModel.TranscribeAudio` returns `openai, no such method`.
6. Repeat with `POST /api/v1/audio/speech` and `model_name: "tts-1"`; the driver returns the same `no such method` response for TTS.

##…

problems.title: [Bug]: GET /api/v1/documents/images/<image_id> fails when thumbnail object key contains hyphens
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

6b41c8c18d246749f5cebdbf5c4edbfab062667f

### RAGFlow image version

6b41c8c18

### Other environment information

```Markdown
- OS type: Linux
- Others: Reproducible from source in `api/apps/restful_apis/document_api.py` (`get_document_image`). Self-hosted / Docker deployments using current `main`.
```

### Actual behavior

`GET /api/v1/documents/images/<image_id>` returns **Image not found** for valid thumbnails when the storage object key contains a hyphen.

Document list/detail APIs expose thumbnail URLs as:

```text
/api/v1/documents/images/{dataset_id}-{thumbnail}
```

In `get_document_image()`, parsing uses:

```python
arr = image_id.split("-")
if len(arr) != 2:
    return get_data_error_result(message="Image not found.")
bkt, nm = image_id.split("-")
```

`dataset_id` is a 32-character hex string (no hyphens), but `thumbnail` is the MinIO/storage object name and often includes hyphens (e.g. `page-1.png`, `img-preview.jpg`). Example `image_id`:

```text
<kb_id>-page-1.png
```

`split("-")` yields more than two segments, so the handler rejects the request even when the blob exists in storage.

### Expected behavior

Parse `image_id` by splitting only on the **first** hyphen between bucket (`dataset_id` / `kb_id`) and object key:

```python
parts = image_id.split("-", 1)
if len(parts) != 2:
    return get_data_error_result(message="Image not found.")
bkt, nm = parts
```

Thumbnail URLs from document APIs should load for object keys that contain hyphens.

### Steps to reproduce

```Markdown
1. Ensure a document has a non-empty `thumbnail` stored in the knowledge-base bucket, with an object key that contains at least one hyphen (e.g. `page-1.png`).
2. List documents via API and copy the returned `thumbnail` URL, or construct:  
   `GET /api/v1/documents/images/<kb_id>-page-1.png`
3. Request that URL (browser or `curl`).
4. Observe response: **Image not found.**
5. Confirm the object exists in storage under bucket `<kb_id>` and key `page-1.png` while the API parser fails because `len(image_id.split("-")) > 2`.
```

### Additional information

- Affected code: `api/apps/restful_apis/document_api.py` — `get_document_image()` (approx. lines 1618–1651).
- Related but distinct: #14763 (missing auth / cross-tenant concerns on the same route).
- Optional follow-up: the handler always sets `Content-Type: image/JPEG` even for PNG/WebP; consider inferring MIME from the object key extension like other d…

problems.title: [Bug]: id2image() fails to load chunk images when storage object key contains hyphens
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

14649a2483c19e0f063ccc388d743c7b0dd6136e

### RAGFlow image version

14649a248

### Other environment information

```Markdown
- OS type: Linux
- Others: Reproducible from source in `rag/utils/base64_image.py` (`image2id` / `id2image`). Affects chunk indexing and retrieval when image object names include hyphens.
```

### Actual behavior

Chunk and document images are stored with a composite ID:

```python
d["img_id"] = f"{bucket}-{objname}"
```

(`image2id` in `rag/utils/base64_image.py`)

When loading the image later, `id2image()` parses the ID with:

```python
arr = image_id.split("-")
if len(arr) != 2:
    return
bkt, nm = image_id.split("-")
```

If `objname` contains hyphens (e.g. `page-1.jpg`, `fig-preview.png`), `split("-")` produces more than two segments, `len(arr) != 2`, and **`id2image` returns `None` silently**. Chunk previews and any code path that resolves `img_id` to a PIL image fail even though the blob exists in storage.

This is the same parsing mistake as #15115 (`GET /api/v1/documents/images/<image_id>`), but in the **RAG indexing/retrieval** path rather than the HTTP thumbnail endpoint.

### Expected behavior

Parse the composite ID with `split("-", 1)` so only the first hyphen separates bucket from object key:

```python
parts = image_id.split("-", 1)
if len(parts) != 2 or not parts[0] or not parts[1]:
    return
bkt, nm = parts
```

Images stored via `image2id` should load correctly when `objname` contains hyphens.

### Steps to reproduce

```Markdown
1. Index a document/chunk where `image2id` is called with an `objname` that contains a hyphen (or inspect an existing chunk whose stored `img_id` looks like `<kb_id>-page-1.jpg`).
2. Call `id2image(img_id, storage_get_func)` (or trigger retrieval/UI that displays chunk images from `img_id`).
3. Observe `None` is returned with no image, while the object exists at bucket `<kb_id>` and key `page-1.jpg`.
4. Compare with `image_id.split("-", 1)` which correctly yields `("<kb_id>", "page-1.jpg")`.
```

### Additional information

- Affected code: `rag/utils/base64_image.py` — `id2image()` (lines ~96–109); IDs are created in `image2id()` (line ~89).
- Related: #15115 (HTTP `get_document_image` uses the same flawed `split("-")` pattern; fix both for consistency).
- Suggested fix: extract a shared `_parse_storage_composite_id(image_id)` helper used by `id2image` and `get_document_image`.

problems.title: [Bug]: ZhipuAI Go driver advertises ASR model but returns no such method
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues, including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report.
- [x] Non-English title submissions will be closed directly.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

57ddd7918

### RAGFlow image version

main branch / Go API server

### Other environment information

- Component: Go model provider layer
- Provider: ZhipuAI
- Model: `glm-asr-2512`

### Actual behavior

The ZhipuAI model config advertises `glm-asr-2512` as an ASR model, but the Go driver does not implement ASR transcription. Calling `TranscribeAudio` on the ZhipuAI Go driver returns `zhipu, no such method`.

The config also lacks an `asr` URL suffix for the documented Z.AI audio transcription endpoint.

### Expected behavior

The ZhipuAI Go driver should support ASR for `glm-asr-2512` by sending a multipart request to the provider's audio transcription endpoint and returning the transcribed text.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use the Go model provider layer with provider `zhipu-ai`.
2. Select the configured ASR model `glm-asr-2512`.
3. Call `TranscribeAudio` with a valid API key and an audio file.
4. The driver returns `zhipu, no such method` instead of calling the provider ASR endpoint.

### Additional information

Current config advertises the ASR model:

```json
{
  "name": "glm-asr-2512",
  "max_tokens": 4096,
  "model_types": ["asr"]
}
```

Current Go driver implementation:

```go
func (o *ZhipuAIModel) TranscribeAudio(...) (*ASRResponse, error) {
    return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s, no such method", o.Name())
}
```

Z.AI documents `glm-asr-2512` at `POST /api/paas/v4/audio/transcriptions` with multipart fields `model`, `stream`, and `file`:
https://docs.z.ai/api-reference/audio/audio-transcriptions

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement OCR in the ZhipuAI Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. The ZhipuAI Go driver advertises the `glm-ocr` model as an OCR model in `conf/models/zhipu-ai.json`, but `ZhipuAIModel.OCRFile` in `internal/entity/models/zhipu-ai.go` still returns `"zhipu, no such method"`.

Z.AI documents GLM-OCR as a supported layout parsing/OCR API. The documented endpoint is:

POST https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4/layout_parsing

with request body fields `model: "glm-ocr"` and `file`, where `file` can be an image/PDF URL or base64 content. The response includes `md_results`, the recognized OCR result in Markdown format.

Because the Go driver currently has no OCR implementation, a tenant who selects the built-in `glm-ocr` model cannot actually run OCR through the Go model layer even though the model is visible in the provider config.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Implement `ZhipuAIModel.OCRFile` so the advertised `glm-ocr` model works end to end in the Go model layer.

The driver should:

- Add the ZhipuAI OCR URL suffix to `conf/models/zhipu-ai.json` as `"ocr": "layout_parsing"`.
- Validate API key, model name, and input file URL/content before making an HTTP request.
- POST to `${BaseURL}/${URLSuffix.OCR}` with JSON body:
  ```json
  {
    "model": "glm-ocr",
    "file": "<url-or-base64>"
  }
  ```
- Send `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` and `Content-Type: application/json`.
- Accept either a file URL or raw file bytes. Raw file bytes should be base64 encoded for the documented `file` field.
- Parse `md_results` from the response and return it through the existing `OCRFileResponse.Text` field.
- Return clear errors for non-200 responses, invalid JSON, or missing `md_results`.

No new public API and no interface changes are required.

### Describe implementation you've considered

Follow the existing provider pattern used by other Go model drivers: resolve region/base URL, append the configured URL suffix, send a JSON request with Bearer auth, parse the provider-specific response into the shared `OCRFileResponse` type, and cover request/response behavior with focused `httptest` unit tests.

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
No user-facing documentation changes are required. ZhipuAI already appears in the model provider config and already lists `glm-ocr` as an OCR model.

Use case: a tenant configures ZhipuAI as a model provider, selects `glm-ocr` for OCR, and asks RAGFlow to extract text from an image or PDF…

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement provider: AWS Bedrock in Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. AWS Bedrock is listed as unchecked in the Go-rewrite tracker #14736 with no referenced PR, and conf/llm_factories.json:1566` already registers a `Bedrock` factory (`tags: "LLM,TEXT EMBEDDING"`, `rank: "860"`, empty `llm: []`) — but `internal/entity/models/` has no `bedrock.go` driver and `conf/models/` has no `bedrock.json`, so the new Go API server cannot route AWS Bedrock instances. The Python side already supports Bedrock through the unified `LiteLLMBase` dispatcher (`rag/llm/chat_model.py:1295` lists `"Bedrock"` in `_FACTORY_NAME`, and rag/llm/chat_model.py:1872-1900` implements both access-key and IAM-role authentication paths via boto3 STS `AssumeRole`).
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Add chat support for AWS Bedrock as a new `BedrockModel` in `internal/entity/models/bedrock.go`, plus `conf/models/bedrock.json`, `bedrock_test.go`, and a factory registration in `internal/entity/models/factory.go`.

Bedrock is an AWS-signed (SigV4) REST API rather than an OpenAI-compatible surface, so this driver differs from the SaaS cluster (Astraflow, Avian, Novita, TogetherAI, Replicate, DeepInfra, Upstage, LongCat). It should mirror the Python implementation’s auth model:

- **API key format:** JSON blob with `auth_mode` ∈ {`access_key_secret`, `iam_role`, `assume_role`}, `bedrock_region`, and either `bedrock_ak`/`bedrock_sk` or `aws_role_arn`. Same shape as `rag/llm/chat_model.py:1878`+.
- **Endpoints (region-scoped):**
  - chat (non-streaming): `POST https://bedrock-runtime.{region}.amazonaws.com/model/{modelId}/converse`
  - chat (streaming): `POST https://bedrock-runtime.{region}.amazonaws.com/model/{modelId}/converse-stream` (event-stream framing, not SSE)
  - list models: `GET https://bedrock.{region}.amazonaws.com/foundation-models`
- **Auth:** AWS SigV4 with service `bedrock` / `bedrock-runtime`. For `iam_role`, call STS `AssumeRole` first and use the temporary credentials (mirroring `rag/llm/chat_model.py:1888-1895`).
- **Connection check:** delegate to `ListModels`.

Initial scope is chat-only (matching how Novita, NVIDIA, TogetherAI, and Replicate landed); Embedding and Image2Text (Titan / Cohere on Bedrock) belong in follow-on issues.


### Describe implementation you've considered

Use the official AWS SDK for Go v2 (`github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/bedrockruntime` and `.../bedrock`) so SigV4 signing, retry policy, and event-stream decoding are…

problems.title: [Bug]: JWT algorithm confusion in OIDC login enables pre-auth account takeover
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

e6dd39753

### RAGFlow image version

v0.25.5 / nightly @ `main` (commit `e6dd39753`)

### Other environment information

- Hardware parameters: any
- OS type: any (Linux verified)
- Others: reproduces on any RAGFlow deployment with OIDC login enabled. The
  bug is in the Python OIDC client, not in any hardware- or OS-specific path.

### Actual behavior

`OIDCClient.parse_id_token` in
[`api/apps/auth/oidc.py`](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/blob/main/api/apps/auth/oidc.py)
reads the JWT signing algorithm from the **unverified JWT header** and passes
it through to `jwt.decode(..., algorithms=[alg], ...)` as the trust anchor:

```python
def parse_id_token(self, id_token):
    try:
        # Decode JWT header without verifying signature
        headers = jwt.get_unverified_header(id_token)

        # OIDC usually uses `RS256` for signing
        alg = headers.get("alg", "RS256")

        # Use PyJWT's PyJWKClient to fetch JWKS and find signing key
        jwks_cli = jwt.PyJWKClient(self.jwks_uri)
        signing_key = jwks_cli.get_signing_key_from_jwt(id_token).key

        # Decode and verify signature
        decoded_token = jwt.decode(
            id_token,
            key=signing_key,
            algorithms=[alg],
            audience=str(self.client_id),
            issuer=self.issuer,
        )
        return decoded_token
    except Exception as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Error parsing ID Token: {e}")
```

This is the textbook **JWT algorithm-confusion** vulnerability (CWE-345 /
CWE-347, exemplified by CVE-2015-2951, CVE-2016-10555, CVE-2018-1000531,
CVE-2022-29217, and many more). There are two distinct exploitation paths,
both leading to **pre-auth account takeover**:

**1. `alg: "none"` → signature verification bypass**

The attacker constructs a JWT whose header is `{"alg": "none", "typ": "JWT"}`
and whose body is `{"sub": "victim-subject", "email": "victim@example.com",
"iss": "<configured-issuer>", "aud": "<configured-client-id>", "exp": ...,
"iat": ...}` with no signature segment. They present this token on the OIDC
callback. The handler:

1. `jwt.get_unverified_header(id_token)` returns `{"alg": "none", ...}`
2. `alg = "none"` (line 69)
3. `jwt.decode(id_token, key=signing_key, algorithms=["none"], audience=...,
   issuer=...)` — because `algorithms` contains `"none"`, PyJWT's
   `NoneAlgorithm` accepts the token without checking the signature
4. The body is returned and t…

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Slack data source connector
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
RAGFlow currently has no usable Slack data source. A lot of a team's institutional knowledge lives in Slack channels and threads, but there is no way to index that content into a knowledge base today, so users have to export and upload it manually.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

A Slack data source that ingests channels, messages, and threads into a knowledge base, with incremental sync and configuration through the data-source settings UI. 
Users connect with a Slack OAuth token, optionally filter which channels to sync, and have new messages picked up on subsequent syncs.

### Describe implementation you've considered

A connector implementation already exists in the codebase (`common/data_source/slack_connector.py`, ~663 LOC) but is not wired into the sync pipeline or exposed in the UI. Completing it involves:
1. `rag/svr/sync_data_source.py` — implement `Slack._generate()` (currently `pass`): instantiate `SlackConnector`, load credentials, drive its iteration, and yield document batches. The `Confluence` / `Dropbox` wrappers are good models.
2. `common/data_source/__init__.py` — export `SlackConnector`.
3. `web/src/pages/user-setting/data-source/constant/index.tsx` — add the Slack enum value (currently commented out), form fields (OAuth token, optional channel filter), default values, and display metadata + icon.
4. Tests for credential loading and document generation.

`FileSource.SLACK` and `DocumentSource.SLACK` are already defined.

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
Internal support and Q&A teams can ground answers in past Slack discussions; onboarding knowledge captured in channels becomes searchable. Scopes needed:
```

### Additional information

_No response_

problems.title: [Feature Request]: SharePoint data source connector
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
RAGFlow cannot currently index documents stored in Microsoft SharePoint. Many organizations keep their primary document libraries in SharePoint, and without a connector those files can't be brought into a knowledge base without manual export.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

A SharePoint data source that enumerates sites and document libraries and downloads files into a knowledge base, with incremental sync and (ideally) permission/ACL sync so access in RAGFlow mirrors SharePoint. Configured from the data-source settings UI using Microsoft Graph app credentials.

### Describe implementation you've considered

A connector stub already exists (`common/data_source/sharepoint_connector.py`, ~119 LOC). `load_credentials()` builds an MSAL confidential client and `GraphClient`/`ClientContext`, but the document-loading methods are unimplemented. 
Completing it involves:
1. `sharepoint_connector.py` — implement `load_from_checkpoint()` (page through document libraries via Graph), slim-doc retrieval for permission sync, `build_dummy_checkpoint()`, `validate_checkpoint_json()`, and `validate_connector_settings()`. Map SharePoint ACLs to `ExternalAccess`.
2. `rag/svr/sync_data_source.py` — implement `SharePoint._generate()` (currently `pass`).
3. `common/data_source/__init__.py` — export `SharePointConnector`.
4. `web/src/pages/user-setting/data-source/constant/index.tsx` — add the enum value (currently commented out), form fields (tenant_id, client_id, client_secret, site_url), defaults, display metadata + icon.
5. Tests.

`FileSource.SHAREPOINT` is already defined.

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
Enterprises with SharePoint-based document management can index policies, manuals, and project docs directly. Requires `Sites.Read.All` / `Files.Read.All` application permissions with admin consent.
```

### Additional information

_No response_

problems.title: [Bug]: Add custom model endpoint can panic on empty model_types and ignores path provider fields
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

`8d8ea718779d3e9498768ab73a5c6b18035a2262`

### RAGFlow image version

`main` branch at commit `8d8ea718779d3e9498768ab73a5c6b18035a2262`; not image-version specific.

### Other environment information

- Hardware parameters: N/A
- OS type: N/A
- Others: Reproducible from `internal/handler/providers.go`, `internal/service/model_service.go`, and the provider route in `internal/router/router.go`.

### Actual behavior

The add-custom-model route includes both provider and instance in the URL:

```go
POST /api/v1/providers/:provider_name/instances/:instance_name/models
```

However, `AddCustomModel` does not read `provider_name` or `instance_name` from the path. It validates and uses only the JSON body fields `provider_name` and `instance_name`.

This causes two practical problems:

1. A request can target `/providers/openai/instances/default/models` but put a different provider or instance in the JSON body. The service uses the body values, not the URL target.
2. The handler only checks `req.ModelTypes == nil`. An empty array such as `"model_types": []` passes validation, then `ModelProviderService.AddCustomModel` indexes `request.ModelTypes[0]`, causing an index-out-of-range panic.

### Expected behavior

The endpoint should use `provider_name` and `instance_name` from the path, or explicitly reject requests where body values do not match the path target.

It should also reject missing or empty `model_types` before the service indexes the slice. Invalid input should return a normal bad-request response instead of a panic.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run RAGFlow from `main` at commit `8d8ea718779d3e9498768ab73a5c6b18035a2262`.
2. Send a request like:

```bash
curl -X POST "http://localhost:<port>/api/v1/providers/openai/instances/default/models" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "provider_name": "openai",
    "instance_name": "default",
    "model_name": "empty-types-model",
    "model_types": [],
    "max_tokens": 8192
  }'
```

3. Observe that the handler accepts the request because `model_types` is non-nil.
4. Observe that the service indexes `request.ModelTypes[0]` and can panic with an index-out-of-range error.
5. Send the same URL with a JSON body whose `provider_name` or `instance_name` points somewhere else. The handler uses the body values instead of the URL path values.

### Additional information

I searched open and closed issues/PRs for related terms including `Add…

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement ListModels in the VolcEngine Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Note: this issue was originally filed for Aliyun Balance. After verifying that the
DashScope endpoint specified in that earlier framing returns 404 (it does not exist
on DashScope, and account balance is exposed only via the separate BSS OpenAPI which
uses AccessKey + HMAC-SHA256 signing rather than the model API key), I am repurposing
this issue for a different concrete gap in the same Go-driver track.

The VolcEngine Go driver in internal/entity/models/volcengine.go ships a stub
ListModels method that always returns "volcengine, no such method". Other Go drivers
that have already landed in the recent Go-rewrite effort (DeepSeek, Gitee, NVIDIA,
OpenAI, SiliconFlow) all implement ListModels against the OpenAI-compatible /models
endpoint, and the model picker UI relies on it.

VolcEngine (Doubao / Ark) does expose a documented OpenAI-compatible models endpoint
that accepts the same Bearer token used for chat and embedding requests:

  GET https://ark.cn-beijing.volces.com/api/v3/models

Without ListModels, a tenant who configures VolcEngine has no in-product way to see
which endpoints are available on their Ark account, even though the chat and
embedding paths in the same driver are already working.

conf/models/volcengine.json also does not yet declare a "models" entry under
url_suffix, so even if the method body were filled in there is no URL to call.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Implement `VolcEngine.ListModels` in `internal/entity/models/volcengine.go` and add the
matching `models` URL suffix to `conf/models/volcengine.json` so the existing model
picker works for VolcEngine the same way it already works for the other drivers in this
package.

The driver should:

1. Resolve the region with a default-region fallback, the same way the surrounding
   VolcEngine methods already do.
2. GET `${BaseURL[region]}/${URLSuffix.Models}`, which resolves to
   `https://ark.cn-beijing.volces.com/api/v3/models` with the default region.
3. Send `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` when an API key is configured. Mirror the
   existing NVIDIA `ListModels` behavior of omitting the header when the key is empty,
   so an unauthenticated caller still gets a clear `401 Unauthorized` from upstream.
4. Surface non-200 responses with the upstream status line and body, the same shape
   the other Go drivers use.
5. Parse the JSON response via the package-level `DSModelList` / `DSModel`…

problems.title: [Feature Request]: Implement provider: Tencent Hunyuan in Go driver
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Yes. Tencent Hunyuan is listed as unchecked in the Go-rewrite tracker #14736, and `conf/llm_factories.json:3830` already registers a `Tencent Hunyuan` factory (`tags: "LLM,IMAGE2TEXT"`) with `"url": "https://api.hunyuan.cloud.tencent.com/v1"` and 5 preconfigured chat models (hunyuan-pro, hunyuan-standard, …) — but `internal/entity/models/` has no `hunyuan.go` driver and `conf/models/` has no `hunyuan.json`, so the new Go API server cannot route Tencent Hunyuan instances. The Python side already supports Tencent Hunyuan through the unified `LiteLLMBase` dispatcher (`rag/llm/chat_model.py:1265`+, enum `HunYuan` in `rag/llm/__init__.py:60`, default base `https://api.hunyuan.cloud.tencent.com/v1`, LiteLLM prefix `openai/`).
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Add chat support for Tencent Hunyuan as a new `HunyuanModel` in `internal/entity/models/hunyuan.go`, plus `conf/models/hunyuan.json`, `hunyuan_test.go`, and a factory registration in `internal/entity/models/factory.go`. Tencent Hunyuan exposes an OpenAI-API-compatible REST surface (https://cloud.tencent.com/document/product/1729/111007):

- chat: `${base}/v1/chat/completions` with `{model, messages, stream}` and `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. SSE stream parsing.
- list models: `${base}/v1/models`.
- check connection: delegate to ListModels.

Base URL defaults to `https://api.hunyuan.cloud.tencent.com/v1` with user-supplied override, matching the existing SaaS Go drivers: Astraflow (#15062), Avian (#15044), Novita (#14850), TogetherAI (#14957), Replicate (#14958), DeepInfra (#14978), Upstage (#14819), LongCat (#14809).
Follow-on issues will add Image2Text (`IMAGE2TEXT` is in the factory tags), in line with how Novita, NVIDIA, TogetherAI, and other providers landed method-by-method.

### Describe implementation you've considered

_No response_

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
No new docs needed. Makes Add-Provider work for Tencent Hunyuan through the Go
API server, the same way it already works for Astraflow, Avian, Novita,
TogetherAI, Replicate, DeepInfra, Upstage, and LongCat.
```

### Additional information

Tracked under #14736. SaaS-provider cluster: Astraflow #15062, Avian #15044, Novita #14850, TogetherAI #14957, Replicate #14958, DeepInfra #14978, Upstage #14819, LongCat #14809. `conf/llm_factories.json:3830` already declares the `Tencent Hunyuan` factory with 5 preconfigured chat models …

problems.title: [Bug]: Failed to encode response: json: unsupported value: NaN (status code: 500)
problems.issue_body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

1234a567b

### RAGFlow image version

0.25.2

### Other environment information

```Markdown

```

### Actual behavior

When I invoke the "converse with chat assistant" interface for streaming response, sometimes the following error occurs: "Failed to encode response: json: unsupported value: NaN (status code: 500)". Moreover, when asking the same question repeatedly, sometimes the response is normal, but sometimes the above error will be reported.

### Expected behavior

I hope to receive the results in a normal streaming manner and without any errors.

### Steps to reproduce

```Markdown
interface:api/v1/chat/completions
parameter:
{
    "messages":[
            {
                "role":"user",
                "content":"我的问题"
            }
        ],
    "stream":true,
    "chat_id":"my_chat_id",
    "session_id":"my_session_id"
}

When using the above interface information to make the call, the streaming interface occasionally returns an error: "Failed to encode response: json: unsupported value: NaN (status code: 500)"
The current embedding model in use is bge-m3 from ollama.And the parsed document is also completely parsed.
```

### Additional information

The specific log information is as follows:
data:{"code": 500, "message": "failed to encode response: json: unsupported value: NaN (status code: 500)", "data": {"answer": "**ERROR**: failed to encode response: json: unsupported value: NaN (status code: 500)", "reference": []}}
data:{"code": 0, "message": "", "data": true}

problems.title: [Subtask]: Go API — Data Source (Connector) management for the user-setting Data Source page
problems.issue_body:
### Parent Issue

Parent issue: #15240

### Detail of Subtask

Port the connector (data source) management endpoints that power `web/src/pages/user-setting/data-source/` from Python to Go. Only `GET /connectors` (list) is implemented in Go today; the rest of the lifecycle is missing.

APIs to implement (base path `/api/v1`), Python source `api/apps/restful_apis/connector_api.py`:

| Method | Path | Description | Go status |
|--------|------|-------------|-----------|
| GET | `/connectors` | List connectors for the tenant | ✅ done (`ListConnectors`) |
| POST | `/connectors` | Create a connector | ❌ missing |
| GET | `/connectors/{connector_id}` | Get one connector (config + sync state) | ❌ missing |
| PATCH | `/connectors/{connector_id}` | Update a connector | ❌ missing |
| DELETE | `/connectors/{connector_id}` | Delete a connector | ❌ missing |
| GET | `/connectors/{connector_id}/logs` | List sync logs for a connector | ❌ missing |
| POST | `/connectors/{connector_id}/rebuild` | Trigger a full re-sync / rebuild | ❌ missing |
| POST | `/connectors/{connector_id}/test` | Validate credentials / test connection | ❌ missing |

Out of scope (track separately): the provider OAuth web flows (`/connectors/google/oauth/web/start`, `/connectors/gmail|google-drive/oauth/web/callback`, `/connectors/box/oauth/web/*`) — these involve external provider redirects and state and should be their own subtask.

### Describe implementation you've considered

Follow the existing handler → service → dao layering (reference PR #14790).

- `internal/dao/connector.go` **already implements** `Create`, `GetByID`, `UpdateByID`,   `DeleteByID`, `ListByTenantID`, `ListByDatasetID` — so the CRUD endpoints are mostly   wiring + request/response mapping.
- `internal/service/connector.go` currently has only `ListConnectors`; add `Create/Get/Update/Delete/Logs/Rebuild/Test` service methods.
- `internal/handler/connector.go` currently has only `ListConnectors`; add the new handlers and register them in `internal/router/router.go` under the `/connectors` group (and the legacy `/v1/connector` group as needed).
- Sync logs read from the existing sync-log table (mirror the Python query); `rebuild` enqueues a sync task; `test` calls the connector's validation path.
